comments: I love how editing team saw earwax at 1:37 and said "lets upload it"
I’ve had the pros  2 for over two years, and they haven’t skipped a beat. They are honestly worth every penny.
When I was this early my wife got pregnant
Got some leather cover that gotta say is phenomenal, my airpods are looking so nice
Everyone is talking about the earwax, but I just appreciate Marques keeping it real
As a musician, the transparency mode is great for practicing with a background track and being able to hear a more accurate version of how I sound when compared to what I think I sound like.

I’ve had my AirPods Pro 2 go thru not one. Not 2. But three full rounds in the washer machine. By mistake leaving them in my pockets without checking and let them dry over night. Came back to life within 24-48 hours. They arent fully water resistant but they can 100% come back to life with the proper aftercare. That’s some good tech.

Marques, I am a senior citizen with a significant hearing loss. I used the AP 2's for the past couple of years with good results, BUT the AP 3's are a brand new level of help, and I can't say enough about the new sound and fit. Bravo Apple! Well done.
1:40 surely clean the old ones lol
6:52 average next cubicle employee
When i saw how clean the AirPods Pro 2's were at 1:40, I knew half of the comments were going to be about it
My pro 2 still rocking, still a lot of worth. If anything, maybe cover them with something because they've lost a bit of luster
There's an apple engineer that worked on this design somewhere cheering that they got the first airpod that stays in his ears.
1:40 maaan clean your AirPods before giving us 8K b-roll of your earwax
2:10 standing over a grate holding an AirPod. That’s the level of self confidence I strive for.

The ANC is my favourite part of these new pods. It's honestly insane when you turn it on and hear little to nothing but the sound of your music, podcast, or the video I'm watching right now, it amplifies the sound quality because you can clearly read the song.

I’ve had mine since 2020. Long painful runs in the rain, sun and snow, multiple washer cycles, breakups, loneliness, happiness, people dying, the case got set on fire (they look like shit), I lost and found them so many times. almost every hike I’ve done alone, almost every bike ride, most days at work, audiobooks, long distance calls at Christmas to my family. The last 5 years of my life my AirPod pros have weirdly been a companion. Like an extension of myself. Like a plug that immerses me into the virtual world  in my ear. It sounds corny but it’s true. They’re still going. The batteries are getting a bit weaker. When they die or the battery gets unbearable I’ll definitely get the new ones.

finally retiring the OG airpods pro from 2019. You have done your job all these years.
Airpods pro 3 arriving friday!

Update: Waw The sound difference is incredible. you definitely hear the difference. It's just so crisp compared to the old ones. love it! 

Will be testing ANC next week on the plane :D
Sometimes the ‘small’ changes like dust resistance outlast the flashy features, longevity is an underrated part of innovation.
I covered my AP with some real nice piece of leather and I regret nothing. Pretty good

ranked.transcript: When the initial AirPods Pro 3 reviews came out, every major reviewer basically said the same thing. The sound quality was excellent and the noise cancellation was quite improved from the AirPods Pro 2. But all of them ended up missing the biggest downside that I've run into with the AirPods Pro 3, which didn't show up until several weeks after launch, which is exactly why I review things over the long term, because some issues just don't show up in 2 weeks. So, what is this big issue with the AirPods Pro 3? Well, a few weeks into using them constantly, I started to notice weird background sounds while using ANC. It sounds kind of hollow, almost like this oceanlike noise, as if there was like just some sound leakage problem with the AirPods Pro 3. And I'd notice this with the sound of my own voice when I'm writing at my Mac and reading out scripts to myself or the noise of just typing on a keyboard or weirdly a sound like rustling a plastic bag. That's something that definitely triggers it as well. Now, all of these were sounds that the AirPods Pro 3 were canceling out fine when I first got them. And even without any active noise, the background noise you hear at times would just sound slightly echoey or hollow as well. Now, at first I thought, well, maybe this is just a fit issue, or maybe the AirPods Pro just got dirty. I did the ear tip test. I actually tried a size up and size down, and the issues still occurred. So, it's clearly not a fit issue. I got the AirPods cleaning kit from Apple and cleaned them following Apple's instructions. That didn't fix it either. Okay, so maybe it's just a software glitch. So, I unpaired them from my iPhone and other Apple devices and I reset them by doubletapping the front of the case continuously, which is how you do it now since there's no Bluetooth pairing button on the back. And that didn't resolve it either. So, at that point, I went to Reddit and sure enough, other users were complaining of similar issues. But given how many AirPods Pro Apple sells, and even though there were numerous posts about this, I figured perhaps all of these people, myself included, we just got faulty pairs of AirPods. So, a few weeks ago, like any sane person would do, I bought another pair of AirPods Pro 3. I took them out of their case, put them in, and everything sounded great. The noise cancellation was perfect for about 45 minutes and then there it was, that hollow background noise again. So, at this point, it's very unlikely that I just got two faulty pairs of AirPods that were manufactured months apart from each other. So, I went back to Reddit and realized I had missed a key detail that someone had pointed out. When this hollow noise appears, if you trigger transparency mode and then switch back to noise cancellation again, it apparently disappears. And sure enough, that was my experience as well, making it likely that this issue that I've been plagued with is actually a software issue. Or at least that's my best guess at this point. And if you've noticed a similar issue with your AirPods Pro 3 or just had the noise cancellation degrade but don't notice that hollow sound as much, do definitely let me know in the comments. And if you want to see more long-term reviews like this one, by the way, a thumbs up on this video does go a long way to supporting us, as well as, of course, making sure you're subscribed. All right, so I can just end the video now and say I don't recommend these, right? It's actually a little bit more complicated than that. Uh, one, this is an issue that I do not think is affecting everybody who bought uh these AirPods. Otherwise, you'd be seeing even way more threads and comments about this issue online. So, either this is an issue that is only affecting certain people's uh AirPods, maybe it's like the internal mics and different people's ear shapes are interacting with them differently. So, that's why some people experience it while others don't. Or this is an issue that is impacting everybody's AirPods, but only certain people notice it or can hear it. With audio, audio is kind of weird. People perceive audio differently. I could also see that argument being plausible as well. But the real reason I just can't say I 100% don't recommend these is even with the issue I've had with them. I find myself still using them quite often because of the highlights like noise cancellation when it's actually working. These buds are quite a bit better than the AirPods Pro 2, blocking out more background noise, especially for higher frequencies that I could actually use them while cooking, which I could never do with the Pro 2 because they didn't block out enough of those higher frequency noises. Overall, I think the AirPods Pro 3 noise cancellation is actually right in line with my new Sony WF1000 XM6s, which I'm currently reviewing. Now, given that the XM 6s don't have that degradation issue that I've had with the AirPods Pro 3, plus they come with foam tips that are all foam tips and just still give you better passive noise isolation overall. I think they still remain the better option for noise cancellation. Now, the AirPods Pro 3 did introduce some foam into their tips, which is part of why their noise cancellation is better than the previous generation, but they still have that classic silicon outer layer. I've noticed with the AirPods Pro 3 specifically, after long listening sessions, I'll notice my ear canal will have just like a tiny layer of condensation. Like not like beaded water or anything like that, but you can just tell that there's a little bit of condensation in there. 해요. 그래서 요것도 세 개를 한번 비교해 봤습니다. 애플 파이너 컷 카메라 2.0 공개. 아이폰 17프로 및 아이폰 17프로맥스의 프로레즈 로우 및 젤락 지원 기능과 신규 아이폰 라인업의 센터 스테이지 전면 카메라를 활용해 크게 도약한다. 전체적으로 새 제품 통합 품질은 괜찮았거든요. 근데 버저 3프로랑 에어팟 프로 2가 조금 먹에 비해 에어팟 프로 3는 가장 선명하게 들렸습니다. 근데 어떻게 보면 전자 느낌이 조금 심하게 느껴지기도 했고요. 그리고 이번에 새롭게 추가된게 아까 말했던 것처럼 이제 신박수 측정 기능이 이번에 새롭게 들어갔잖아요. 그래서 요게 장점이 뭐냐면 이제는 애플워치 없이도 운동 트래킹이 가능합니다. 그래서 요렇게 이제 착용을 하고 나서 우리가 많이 쓰는 피트니스 앱으로 들어갔을 때 iOS 26으로 업데이트가 안 되어서 지금 안 뜨거든요. iOS 26을 업데이트 하면 여기에 새롭게 추가된 기능이 됩니다. 그럼 여기서 애플러워치에서 운동 트래킹 간 것처럼 또 선택을 해서 운동을 할 수 있어요. 예전에 제가 찾아보기론 귀에서 측정하는게 손목보다 더 정확하다고들은 거 같거든요. 그대도 애플에서 물어보기로는 이제 에어팟만 착용하고도 운동 트래킹 신박수나 칼로리 데이터를 받을 수도 있지만 애플로워치와 동시에 착용하게 된다면 둘 중에 더 정확한 데이터를 쓴다고 해요. 에프러워치 시리즈 10 갤럭시 워치 8 에어팟 프로를 각각 다른 기기에 연결하고 10분 정도 뛰었을 때 고차범위가 플러스 마이너스 5 정도로 꽤나 정확했습니다. 나머지는 배터리 타임도 좀 증가했습니다. 유닛만 단독으로 봤을 때 6여시간 사용 가능한게 8시간으로 더 늘어났고요. 심지어 여기서 신박수 측정 기능을 켜더라도 기정보다 30분 더 오래 가는 배터리 타임입니다. 근데 또 케이스 사용 시간은 더 줄어들었어요. 30시간에서 24시간으로 줄어들었거든요. 그래서 요걸 보면 유닛은 약간 두꺼워지고 무게가 늘면서 배터리가 조금 더 늘어난 것 같고 케이스는 커졌는데 무게가 줄면서 배터리 용량을 낮춘 거 같기도 합니다. 근데 이제 실용에서 이제 비교를 하자면 케이스의 배터리가 더 느는게 좋냐, 유닛의 배터리가 더 느는게 좋냐, 요거를 비교하면 유닛이긴 하지만 둘 다 느는게 더 좋긴 해요. 또 한편으로 어떻게 보면 기존에서 6시간 사실 6시간 네대 착용하기는 좀 어렵거든요. 그 사이에 한번 케이스에 넣으면 또 빨리 충전되다 보니까 뭐 요거는 그게 중요하진 않다. 그리고 새롭게 들어간 실시간 번역 기능. 제가 현장에서 봤을 때 보니까 가능한 언어에 한국어가 없었습니다. 야 이거 한국어 추가되려면 또 1년 기다려야 되나 했는데 데모 브리핑에 가니까 한국어로 제가 체험을 해 볼 수 있었어요.요 한국어를 켜잖아요. 그럼 기본적으로 일단 번역을 했을 때 노이즈 캔슬링으로 상대방 목소리를 줄이고요. 번역되는 언어가 내 귀로 들리는 그런 방식이었어요. >> 실제로 한국어 번역도 꽤 잘 됐어요. 그러니까 이게 소통하기 무리 없을 정도. 심지어 실시간 번역을 쓰잖아요. 그럼 아이폰에서 번역에 비면서 또 요거를 문자로 볼 수도 있었고요. 요즘 우리 AI가 많이 발전하면서 LLM이라고 해서 뭐 GPT나 재미나이 보면 내가 처음에 물었던 거 외에도 큰틀을 보면서 앞뒤에 문맥을 파악하면서 대답해 주는 것에 비해 얘는 그냥 우리가 일반 번역을 하는 것처럼 앞뒤에 문맥을 파악하지 못하다 보니까 그 점이 좀 단점이었어요. 그리고 기본적인 H2칩은 동일하지만 광대역 칩이 U1에서 U2로 업그레이드 되었습니다. 요것도 재밌는게 뭐냐면 애플에서 첫 번째 울트라 와이드 밴드 초광대역 칩을 유원이라고 정의를 했어요. 근데 아이폰 15대 요거를 계산하면서 2세대 초광제역 칩이라고 언급을 했거든요. 근데 이번 에어팟 프로 3에서는 또 유트라고 하더라고요. 그러니까 이게 약간 좀 족보가 꼬인 거지. 그래서 스펙도 보잖아요. 그럼 2세대 초광대역치비 유원 대비 최대 세배 인식 거래가 늘어났고 그래서 요것도 2세대 초광대역 칩이 그냥 이번에 유원칩으로 이름이 다시 바뀐게 아닐까 같은 칩이 아닐까 해서 찾아봤거든요. 근데 좀 다른 거 같더라고요. 왜냐면 유원칩을 탑재한 프로 2 대비 1.5배 인식 범위가 더 넓고 향상된 인식은 또 새로운 아이폰 에어 17시리즈에서만 지원한다는 것을 보면 이게 좀이 이상해요. 아무튼 오늘은 많은 분들께서 기다리신 에어팟 프로에 대해서 살펴봤습니다. 전반적인 평가로 하자면 노이즈 캔슬링은 이미 프로투도 좋았기 때문에 미친듯한 체감은 아니었고 오히려 음질적인 부분에서의 체감이 저는 더 크게 다가왔습니다. 그리고 운동하시는 분들은 신박수 측정 기능이 들어갔기 때문에 요즘도 좀 좋을 것 같고요. 특히 제가 아침에 운동 가다 보면 에프러워치를 안 들고 가서 아 신박수 측정해야 되는데 칼로리 측정해야 되는데 이렇게 하면서 다시 집에 갔다가 나오는 케이스도 많았거든요. 근데 에어팟은 보통 운동할 때 음악을 대부분 듣다 보니까 고점도 좀 편해지지 않을까? 다만 한 가지 걱정이라면 착용감의 호보로가 좀 많이 심할 거라는 거. 저는 지금 요렇게 간단하게 체험해 봐도 프로 3를 끼울 때마다 큐가 아파. 아무튼 오늘 여기까지요. 아까 말한 것처럼 착용감에서 크게 불편함이었다면 장반적으로 모든 부분에서 업그레이드이다.

judgement.community_consensus: 에어팟 프로3는 음질, 노캔, 신박 센서 등에서 큰 개선이 있었지만 착용감에 대한 호불호가 분명하다. 주로 출퇴근이나 운동 시 사용하는 사람에게 적합하다.
judgement.best_use_case: 출퇴근 시 노이즈 캔슬링을 활용하거나 운동 시 신박 센서와 음악을 함께 사용하는 상황
reviewer_claims: 에어팟 프로3는 음질, 노캔, 신박 센서에서 큰 개선
유닛 무게 증가와 케이스 배터리 감소
착용감 호불호가 분명
실시간 번역 기능은 한국어 지원 제한
community_reactions: 노이즈 캔슬링과 음질에 대한 긍정적 평가

So, in this video, let's talk all things AirPods Pro 3. My experience, what I love, what I think people should know about, and finally, is it worth your hard-earned money, especially now that it's only $199 on Amazon? Let's get into it. But now, before we continue, if you do enjoy videos like this one where we talk all things Apple products, definitely consider subscribing to the channel. But now, let's talk about the first thing, which is going to be what they changed compared to the Pro 2s. So, to start off, let's talk about the design as well as the fitment of the AirPods Pro 3 because they have changed both on the case size as well as the actual earbuds themselves. The shape is just a little bit different compared to years past of the AirPods Pro. So, let's start off with the case itself because there are some highlighting differences that you need to know about. First and foremost, I am using Pitaka's latest case for the AirPods Pro 3 if you guys do want to check it out. A case for your case, some might say. Hey everyone, I need to address something important.
I'm seeing comments pointing out that the song samples don't match what I described in the review, and you're absolutely right to call that out. This is precisely the kind of feedback that makes this community so valuable.

Here's what happened:
To Apple's credit, the AirPods Pro lineup features some of the most sophisticated adaptive audio technology available. We're talking multiple layers of real-time EQ adjustments based on how loud you're listening to them, the specific shape of your ear, ear-tip seal optimization, where it adjusts the sound if the AirPods' eartip shifts, and it does this multiple times a second, all at once.

It's engineering wizardry for listeners, but it creates a freaking nightmare for people to capture consistent frequency responses and samples. That's one of the reasons why all the frequency responses you see online for AirPods Pro often look so different from each other. It's HARD.

For the APP2 samples specifically, it appears the older adaptive algorithms struggled with my testing setup (even with the "fake ear" techniques I use). This created an unintended harshness in the captures that doesn't reflect the real listening experience. Ironically, the APP3's improved processing handled the non-ideal conditions better, adapting the EQ to its intended sound, which means the captures ended up representing the opposite of what you'd actually hear with the APP2, but more accurately with the APP3.

I take full responsibility for not catching this before publishing. When the capture doesn't match reality, I should have either found another solution or been more explicit about the limitation up front. 

What I'm doing about it:
The APP3's new architecture actually makes it EASIER to capture accurately, which brings me to some exciting news: I have a massive four-way comparison coming soon. The Apple AirPods Pro 3 vs. Sony XM5 vs. Bose QuietComfort Ultra Earbuds Gen 2 vs. Samsung Galaxy Buds3 Pro. 

And here's the part I'm genuinely excited about: I captured ANC and transparency mode tests on an actual commercial flight. Real-world conditions, no lab simulations. I finally pulled it off (and didn't get kicked off the flight for looking like I was constructing a bomb with all the wires and tape everywhere).

I've built this channel on honest, fair, empathetic, and balanced thoughts, and on constant refinement and improvement of the testing and quality of the videos.

My description of the sound signature in the video reflects extensive real-world listening, but I completely understand if this situation makes you skeptical. All I can ask is that you judge the upcoming comparison on its own merits.

Thank you for holding me accountable. This is what makes the community better, and it's what makes my testing better.

Thanks for watching This is Tech Today.

finalVerdict.summary: Apple AirPods Pro 3는 이전 세대 대비 ANC 성능이 2배 향상되고, IP57 방수 등급과 심박수 측정 기능이 추가되어 실생활 내구성과 기능성에서 큰 발전을 보입니다. 특히, 하이브리드 이어팁 디자인으로 착용감이 개선되어 운동 시에도 안정적으로 사용할 수 있습니다. 그러나 일부 사용자에게는 고역대 소리가 거슬리거나, 음질이 이전 모델보다 떨어진다고 느끼는 경우가 있으며, 사용자 맞춤형 EQ 조정 기능이 부재해 음질 선호도에 따라 선택이 필요합니다. 또한, 안드로이드 사용자에게는 기능 제한이 존재하며, 이어팁 교체가 다소 불편하다는 점도 단점으로 지적되고 있습니다.

finalVerdict.buying_advice: 신규 구매자라면, iPhone 사용자나 ANC 성능을 중시하는 사용자에게 적합합니다. 특히, 운동 시 안정적인 착용감과 방수 기능이 필요하거나, 심박수 측정 기능을 활용하고 싶은 경우 추천됩니다. 다만, 음질에 까다로운 사용자나 안드로이드 사용자는 다른 제품을 고려하는 것이 좋습니다.
